summary: Systems architecture review accepts the M3 administrative burden scaffold. The change stays within sim-core/protocol/test/process scope, keeps sim-core decoupled from React/Pixi/Electron/Node IO, preserves single-writer world mutation, exposes a read-only query boundary, uses explicit serializable M3 runtime inputs, and keeps M3 save rejection intact.
decisions: No additional ADR is requested for this scoped R3 change because it implements an approved M3 scaffold within existing worker authority, command model, polity-control, and no-runtime-database decisions; it does not change command semantics, save compatibility strategy, production dependencies, platform/thread authority, or frozen product architecture.

Keeping state.m3.administrativeDistricts as runtime-only explicit input is acceptable for this task because requestSaveV1 still rejects M3 state and M3-SAVE-RUNTIME-001 remains the proper place for durable DTO, migration, and golden compatibility evidence.

The additive administrative-load formula is accepted as a milestone scaffold constant, not final balance; future tuning should remain deterministic and reviewable before M3 exit comparisons depend on it.

verification.architecture_boundary: PASS: sim-core remains free of React/Pixi/Electron/DOM/Node IO imports; pnpm test architecture guard passed.
verification.single_writer_and_query_boundary: PASS: administrative burden is exposed via sim.list-m3-administrative-burden read model; no command or UI/client path mutates this state directly.
verification.determinism: PASS: canonicalization sorts administrative rows by polityId, districtId, then controlMode; calculations use integer arithmetic, clamps, and explicit tie-breakers; no Math.random or Date.now patterns were found.
verification.schema_and_versioning: PASS_WITH_NOTE: protocol query kind is explicit under GameQuery schemaVersion 1 and M3 runtime fields are explicit. Since M3 is still unsaved, the runtime schema addition is acceptable now; M3-SAVE-RUNTIME-001 must not persist this without explicit DTO/version/migration tests.
verification.scope: PASS: changed and untracked files are confined to allowed task paths; no forbidden paths, accepted ADRs, save-format package, client packages, app packages, or content-source files are modified.
verification.test_coverage: PASS: tests cover monotonic load composition, read-only overload/resource preservation, deterministic canonical ordering, protocol query parsing, and distinct direct/vassal/tribute-only profiles.

tests_run.result: On feat/m3-admin-burden. Modified files are protocol, sim-core, tests, goal/task/thread process files; untracked writer handoff and route message are present.
Node and worker-compatible hashes matched: hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd.
Architecture boundaries passed; deterministic runner passed; root Vitest 20 files/105 tests passed; web Vitest 1 file/3 tests passed.

risks: Formula weights are scaffold constants. Gameplay/balance review should own final tuning before M3 exit comparison claims.
M3 runtime state remains unsaved. M3-SAVE-RUNTIME-001 must add explicit persisted DTO/schema/version/migration/golden evidence before accepting M3 save/load.

Existing process files project/goal-mode-state.json, project/tasks/active/M3-ADMIN-BURDEN-001.json, and project/tasks/thread-registry.json were already part of task coordination changes; reviewer did not alter them.
requested_action: Lead may proceed with integration/next review gate for M3-ADMIN-BURDEN-001.
